requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Branch Authorisation) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Branch Authorisation) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Urban Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'UCBs / banks' and individually as a 'UCB / bank'.
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'UCBs / banks' and individually as a 'UCB / bank'

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'Urban Co-operative Banks' shall mean Primary Co-operative
Banks as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of Banking
Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Administrative Office' or 'Controlling Office' means a corporate, regional,
zonal, or any other office, by whatsoever name called, that exercises control
or oversight functions on units / Banking Outlets / Offices falling under its
jurisdiction and undertakes internal administrative functions including
oversight of bank's own staff and carries out no banking or business
transactions.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Administrative Office' or 'Controlling Office' means a corporate, regional, zonal, or any other office,

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Back Offices' means a Central Processing Centre (CPC) or an Office, by
whatever name called, that exclusively attends to functions such as data
processing, processing of loans, verification and processing of documents,
issuance of cheque books, demand drafts etc.
requirements.object: (CPC) or an Office, by whatever name called, that exclusively attends to functions such as data processing, processing of

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial
parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory mod

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place separate Board-approved policies for the following:
(1) Opening of branches keeping in view the financial health of the UCB, viability
study of the new branches and customer service.
requirements.object: Board-approved policies for the following: (1) Opening of branches keeping in view the financial health of the UCB, viability st

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall be updated
periodically to keep it aligned with revised RBI guidelines.
requirements.object: keep it aligned with revised RBI guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of a bank shall put in place an institutionalised system for periodically
reviewing the implementation of the Business Facilitator (BF) / Business
Correspondent (BC) Model at the Board level.
requirements.object: institutionalised system for periodically reviewing the implementation of the Business Facilitator (BF) / Business Correspondent (BC)

conditions:
if it meets the following
conditions
requirements.full_sentence:
A bank will be considered as fully complying with ECBA if it meets the following
conditions, based on the audited financial statements as of 31 March of the
immediately preceding financial year:
(1) The CRAR should be at least one percentage point above the minimum
CRAR applicable to the bank as on the reference date;
requirements.object: percentage point above the minimum CRAR applicable to the bank as on the reference date
/ Supervisory Action Framework / PCA of RBI at the time of Board resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant / application

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(7) The bank should have at least two professional directors on the Board as
prescribed in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks -
Governance) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: professional directors on the Board as prescribed in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Governance) Directions, 2

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall determine its compliance with the ECBA every year based on the
audited financial statements as of 31 March of the immediately preceding FY and
place it before its Board within 30 days from the date of adoption of the audit
report.
requirements.object: the ECBA every year based on the audited financial statements as of 31 March of the immediately preceding FY and place i

requirements.timeline:
within 15 calendar days from the date of the Board resolution as per the
format given in Annex I
requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall satisfy itself about the compliance of the bank with ECBA
and pass the necessary resolution approving the same and inform the Reserve
Bank within 15 calendar days from the date of the Board resolution as per the
format given in Annex I.
requirements.object: compliance of the bank with ECBA and pass the necessary resolution approving the same and inform the Reserve Bank within 15 c

conditions: In case during the supervisory review
requirements.full_sentence:
In case during the supervisory review, or even otherwise, if a
bank, which has declared itself compliant with ECBA is found to be non-
compliant, the bank shall be subject to appropriate supervisory and / or
enforcement action as deemed fit by the Reserve Bank, including but not limited
to debarment from self-reviewing itself as ECBA compliant for a period of
minimum of one year.
requirements.object: supervisory and / or enforcement action as deemed fit by the Reserve Bank, including but not limited to debarment from self-

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB may extend its area of operation as follows:
Sr.
requirements.object: operation as follows: Sr

requirements.full_sentence:
a) The whole of its
A UCB may extend its area of operation to the whole of
its district of registration without prior permission from
district of
registration
the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: operation to the whole of its district of registration without prior permission from district of registration the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB in compliance with ECBA may extend its area
b) Additional three
districts besides the
of operation to a maximum of three districts of its choice
district of
within its state of registration (other than its district of
registration), without prior permission from the
registration (within
the state of
Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: Additional three districts besides the of operation to a maximum of three districts of its choice district of within its state of r

requirements.full_sentence:
registration)
c) Beyond the districts
A UCB in Tier 2, 3 and 4 {including Salary Earners' Bank
(SEB) meeting the minimum deposit requirement of a
as mentioned at
Tier 2 UCB} in compliance with ECBA may extend its
point 12 (b) above
and within the state
area of operation beyond the districts as mentioned at
of registration
point 12 (b) above and within the state of registration,
subject to prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: (b) above and within the state area of operation beyond the districts as mentioned at of registration point 12 (b) above and within the state of registration

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB would be permitted to extend its area of
operation to a maximum of five districts in a financial
year, subject to the availability of adequate headroom
capital (methodology given in Part C of Annex II)
required for opening at least one branch in each of the
proposed districts.
requirements.object: one branch in each of the proposed districts

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Beyond the state of
A UCB in Tier 3 and 4 (including SEB meeting the
minimum deposit requirement of a Tier 3 UCB) in
registration
compliance with ECBA and having a minimum
assessed net worth (ANW) of ₹50 crore may extend
its area of operation beyond the state of registration,
subject to prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: operation beyond the state of registration

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB would be permitted to extend its area of
operation to a maximum of two states in a financial year,
subject to the availability of adequate headroom capital
(methodology given in Part C of Annex II) required for
opening at least five branches in each proposed state.
requirements.object: five branches in each proposed state

requirements.full_sentence:
Constitution of the Board of Management (BoM) shall be a mandatory condition
for the expansion of the area of operation for UCBs in Tier 2 and above.
requirements.object: for the expansion of the area of operation for UCBs in Tier 2 and above

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs in
Tier I may constitute BoM as a good governance practice.
requirements.object: good governance practice

requirements.full_sentence:
The byelaws of a UCB must contain a provision that for any revision in the area
of operation, except for the area of operations mentioned at Sr.
requirements.object: for any revision in the area of operation

requirements.full_sentence:
12 (a) & (b)
above, prior approval of the Reserve Bank shall be necessary.

Eligible UCBs as
at 12 (c) & (d) above, desirous of extending their area of operation, shall submit
a resolution passed by their general body to this effect and obtain a No Objection
Certificate (NOC) from the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: by their general body to this effect and obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The applicant bank shall submit a
suitable rationale for utilisation of the current area of operation and proposed
extension of the area of operation while applying for NOC.
requirements.object: for utilisation of the current area of operation and proposed extension of the area of operation while applying for NOC

requirements.full_sentence:
After obtaining the NOC, the bank shall get the amended byelaws registered with
the concerned Registrar of Co-operative Societies (RCS) / Central Registrar of
Co-operative Societies (CRCS).
requirements.object: registered with the concerned Registrar of Co-operative Societies (RCS) / Central Registrar of Co-operative Societies (CRCS)

requirements.timeline:
within 15 calendar days of getting
byelaws registered with RCS
requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB shall approach the Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days of getting
byelaws registered with RCS / CRCS (along with a copy of the approval), for
obtaining a revised authorization.
requirements.object: within 15 calendar days of getting byelaws registered with RCS / CRCS (along with a copy of the approval), for obtaining a r

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that the amendment in byelaws
should be in consonance with the NOC granted by the Reserve Bank, wherever
applicable.
requirements.object: amendment in byelaws should be in consonance with the NOC granted by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Such UCBs shall get the amended byelaws registered with RCS
/ CRCS and then approach the Reserve Bank as per process given in paragraph
16 above.
requirements.object: registered with RCS / CRCS and then approach the Reserve Bank as per process given in paragraph 16 above

requirements.full_sentence:
The
reporting of opening a new place of business shall be as per procedure given in
requirements.object: given in

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should ensure that there are no restrictions imposed by the local
development or other authorities for setting up a commercial establishment in the
locality where the new place of business is proposed to be opened.
requirements.object: no restrictions imposed by the local development or other authorities for setting up a commercial establishment in the locality

requirements.full_sentence:
Prior Approval Route: SEBs and other banks (for
opening of branches of more than 15 per cent allowed
under automatic route), which are in compliance with
ECBA, may apply for opening branches by submitting
an Annual Business Plan (ABP), subject to additional
conditions at paras 25 to 28 below.
requirements.object: by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP)

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank in compliance with ECBA may open ECs
b) Opening of an
Extension Counter
under both automatic route and prior approval route,
(EC)
subject to additional conditions at paras 29 to 32
below.
requirements.object: of an Extension Counter under both automatic route and prior approval route, (EC) subject to additional conditions at paras 29 to

requirements.full_sentence:
Prior Approval Route: A bank may apply for opening
ECs by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP).
requirements.object: by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP)

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Opening of a Central
A bank may open such offices within its area of
operation as per its business requirements, without
Processing Centre,
prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: its area of operation as per its business requirements, without Processing Centre, prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Regional Collection
Centre, Retail Asset
Processing Centre,
Service Branch, Back
Office #
A UCB in compliance with ECBA may, at its
e) Opening of a
Controlling Office
discretion, open one Controlling Office for a cluster of
not less than 30 branches within the area of operation,
(Regional / Zonal /
without prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: Opening of a Controlling Office discretion, open one Controlling Office for a cluster of not less than 30 branches within the area of oper

requirements.full_sentence: # Such offices should not be involved in business origination.
requirements.object: origination

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank is eligible to open a branch in its area of operation (including the
upgradation of an extension counter that is in operation for more than three years)
under the automatic route subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank shall be in compliance with ECBA;
requirements.object: ECBA

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank should have sufficient headroom capital (methodology given in Part
C of Annex II);
requirements.object: (methodology given in Part C of Annex II)

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(3) The bank should have constituted a Board of Management (BoM) (wherever
applicable).
requirements.object: of Management (BoM) (wherever applicable)

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall put in place a policy for opening branches, approved by its Board
of Directors.
requirements.object: policy for opening branches, approved by its Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
While formulating the said policy, the bank shall bear in mind its
financial health, viability of the proposed branches and bank's ability to render
satisfactory customer service.
requirements.object: financial health, viability of the proposed branches and bank's ability to render satisfactory customer service

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy must be reviewed periodically to keep
it aligned with the evolving banking landscape and revision, if any, in Reserve
Bank guidelines.
requirements.object: keep it aligned with the evolving banking landscape and revision

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank shall ensure that the proposal for opening of
such branches in a particular financial year, based on the policy, is duly approved
by its Board of Directors.
requirements.object: for opening of such branches in a particular financial year, based on the policy, is duly approved by its Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
SEBs and other UCBs (for opening of branches of more than 15 per cent allowed
under automatic route), which are in compliance with ECBA, may apply for
opening branches under the Prior Approval Route by submission of a single
Annual Business Plan (ABP) (format given in Annex II) in a financial year, along
with application as per Form V of the Banking Regulation (Co-operative Societies)
Rules, 1966.
requirements.object: under the Prior Approval Route by submission of a single Annual Business Plan (ABP) (format given in Annex II) in a financi

requirements.full_sentence: The bank shall have a policy as at para 24.
requirements.object: at para 24

requirements.full_sentence: ABP for a financial year, should be submitted by the bank well in advance.
requirements.object: bank well in advance

requirements.full_sentence:
The approval granted by the
Reserve Bank for opening the branch will be valid till March 31 of concerned
financial year, by which time the branch must be operationalised, or else the
approval will lapse automatically.
requirements.object: the approval will lapse automatically

requirements.full_sentence:
Non-operationalisation of at least 75% of approved
branches within the permitted time would be viewed seriously and may impact
the approvals in future, including debarment from opening new branches for the
next two years.
requirements.object: future, including debarment from opening new branches for the next two years

requirements.full_sentence:
SEB shall comply with the following additional criteria before applying to open
branches under ABP:
(1) The byelaws should not contain provision for giving loans to outsiders (i.e.,
persons who are / were not employees of the institution) by enrolling them as
members / nominal members;
requirements.object: additional criteria before applying to open branches under ABP: (1) The byelaws should not contain provision for giving loan

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) There should at least be 1000 members at a place where the SEB desires to
open a branch.
requirements.object: members at a place where the SEB desires to open a branch

requirements.full_sentence:
An EC may be opened within the premises of educational institutions, big offices,
factories and hospitals of which the co-operative bank concerned is the principal
banker.
requirements.object: premises of educational institutions, big offices, factories and hospitals of which the co-operative bank concerned is the princi

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall obtain a declaration from the institution in which it
proposes to open EC in the format as per Annex IV.
requirements.object: the institution in which it proposes to open EC in the format as per Annex IV

requirements.full_sentence:
Other bankers to such
entities may also open an EC provided it is not considered feasible by the
principal banker and / or the base branch of the principal banker is beyond 10
kilometres from the proposed EC, after obtaining written consent from the
principal banker.
requirements.object: provided it is not considered feasible by the principal banker and / or the base branch of the principal banker is beyond 10 kilometre

requirements.full_sentence:
An EC may also be opened in the residential colonies provided
no other branch / EC is already existing in the colony and no restrictions have
been imposed by the local development or any other authorities for setting up a
commercial establishment in the residential colony / locality.
requirements.object: the residential colonies provided no other branch / EC is already existing in the colony and no restrictions have been imposed by th

requirements.full_sentence:
No extension
counter should be opened in a marketplace, shopping centre, etc.
requirements.object: marketplace, shopping centre, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Base branch of the bank to which the proposed EC is linked should be within a
distance of 10 kilometres.
requirements.object: of 10 kilometres

requirements.full_sentence:
The transactions carried out in the EC should be
incorporated in the accounts of the base branch on a day-to-day basis.
requirements.object: accounts of the base branch on a day-to-day basis

requirements.full_sentence:
While opening an EC, the bank should take into account important factors such
as need, viability, and overall merits of opening the EC, including the bank's ability
to deploy adequate resources and exercise internal controls.
requirements.object: factors such as need, viability, and overall merits of opening the EC, including the bank's ability to deploy adequate res

requirements.full_sentence:
The facilities at an EC should be restricted to:
(1) deposit / withdrawal transactions;
requirements.object: deposit / withdrawal transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may offer all its products and services through the ATM channels,
provided the technology permits and adequate checks are put in place.
requirements.object: and services through the ATM channels

requirements.full_sentence:
The
business transacted at the ATM shall be recorded in the books of the respective
base branch / Centralised Data Centre.
requirements.object: books of the respective base branch / Centralised Data Centre

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the bank may utilize the ATM screens to
display their own products, or products for which it acts as an agent, or customer
service / financial inclusion / cyber safety messages.
requirements.object: to display their own products, or products for which it acts as an agent, or customer service / financial inclusion / cyber sa

requirements.full_sentence:
These guidelines shall also
be applicable to CDMs and CRMs.
requirements.object: CDMs and CRMs

requirements.full_sentence:
The decision to shift a place of business shall be taken by the Board after taking
into account all the relevant factors, including viability, and should be properly
recorded / minuted in the proceedings of the Board meeting.
requirements.object: Board after taking into account all the relevant factors, including viability, and should be properly recorded / minuted in the procee

requirements.full_sentence:
Proper notice should be given to all existing depositors / clients of the branch /
EC through SMS / public notice / letters at least one month in advance of shifting.
requirements.object: existing depositors / clients of the branch / EC through SMS / public notice / letters at least one month in advance of shifting

requirements.full_sentence:
Closing a branch / EC in one city / town and opening in another shall not be
construed as shifting.

However, a bank may shift its office, except Head Office,
from one town / city to another within its area of operation, without prior approval
of the Reserve Bank.

Closure of a
branch / extension counter shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank should not have been placed under any Directions under section
35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (AACS).
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) The bank should not have been placed under any Directions under section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act,

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The decision to close down the branch / EC should be taken by the Board
after taking into account all the relevant factors and should be properly noted
in the official record of proceedings of the Board meeting.
requirements.object: Board after taking into account all the relevant factors and should be properly noted in the official record of proceedings of the Boa

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The bank should give two months' notice in advance to all existing depositors
/ clients of the branch, including through press releases in leading local
newspapers, as well as communicate to each constituent of the branch well
in advance of the closure of the branch.
requirements.object: in advance to all existing depositors / clients of the branch, including through press releases in leading local newspapers, a

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The bank should report to the Reserve Bank, as per the timeline (reporting
format given in Annex III), along with copies of the Board resolution.
requirements.object: Bank, as per the timeline (reporting format given in Annex III), along with copies of the Board resolution

requirements.timeline:
within 15 calendar days of closing the
branch
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The bank should surrender the branch license in original, wherever
applicable, to the Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days of closing the
branch.
requirements.object: in original

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The bank should preserve all the relevant records and make them available
to the Reserve Bank inspection team for scrutiny during inspection.
requirements.object: records and make them available to the Reserve Bank inspection team for scrutiny during inspection
of the Reserve Bank for: (1) Sale of bank's own premises

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are required to obtain approval from the Reserve Bank by submitting the
application through PRAVAAH portal as per the enclosed format given in Annex
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank by submitting the application through PRAVAAH portal as per the enclosed format given in Annex

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should not enter into any firm commitment to the acquisition of
premises for shifting an office thereat until the Reserve Bank's prior approval has
been obtained.
requirements.object: commitment to the acquisition of premises for shifting an office thereat until the Reserve Bank's prior approval has been obtained

conditions:
if a bank has inadvertently made any such
commitment
requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, if a bank has inadvertently made any such
commitment, it should, in its own interest, take steps to cancel or nullify it.
requirements.object: interest, take steps to cancel or nullify it

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly,
a UCB may, with the approval of its Board, formulate a scheme for use of BF /
BC and ensure that the scheme is in strict compliance with the objectives and
parameters laid down in this circular.
requirements.object: of its Board, formulate a scheme for use of BF / BC and ensure that the scheme is in strict compliance with the objectives and par

requirements.full_sentence:
The scheme may be submitted to the
concerned Regional Office of RBI and approval obtained before engaging the BF
/ BC.
requirements.object: concerned Regional Office of RBI and approval obtained before engaging the BF / BC

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB fulfilling the above conditions may approach the Regional Office, RBI for
permission to engage BF / BC.
requirements.object: RBI for permission to engage BF / BC

requirements.full_sentence:
Under the BF model, the UCB may use intermediaries, such as, Non-Government
Organisations (NGO) / Micro Finance Institutions (MFI) set up under Societies /
Trust Acts, Farmers' Clubs, co-operative societies other than primary & credit co-
operatives, community based organisations, IT enabled rural outlets of corporate
entities, Post Offices, insurance agents, well functioning Panchayats, Village
Knowledge Centres, Agri Clinics / Agri Business Centers, Krishi Vigyan Kendras
and KVIC / KVIB units, and individuals as BFs depending on the comfort level of
the UCB, for providing facilitation services.
requirements.object: Non-Government Organisations (NGO) / Micro Finance Institutions (MFI) set up under Societies / Trust Acts, Farmers' Clubs

requirements.full_sentence:
The facilitation services may include : (i) identification of borrowers and fitment of
activities;
requirements.object: of borrowers and fitment of activities

requirements.full_sentence:
Under the BC Model, NGOs / MFIs set up under Societies / Trust Acts, Co-
operative Societies registered under Mutually Aided Co-operative Societies Acts
or the Co-operative Societies Acts of States, other than primary / co-operative
credit societies, Post Offices, retired bank employees, ex-servicemen, retired
teachers, retired government employees, individual kirana / medical / fair price
shop owners, Individual Public Call Office (PCO) operators, agents of small
savings schemes of Government of India / Insurance Companies, individuals who
own petrol pumps, authorised functionaries of well-run Self Help Groups (SHGs)
linked to the UCB or any other individual including those operating Common
Service Centres may act as BCs.

The UCB may also engage companies registered under
Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956 provided that the companies registered
under Section 25 are stand-alone entities or Section 25 companies in which
NBFCs, banks, telecom companies and other corporate entities or their holding
companies do not have equity holdings in excess of 10 per cent.
requirements.object: Section 25 are stand-alone entities or Section 25 companies in which NBFCs, banks, telecom companies and other corporat

requirements.full_sentence:
If the UCB in
North Eastern Region intend to engage as BCs any other organisation /
association not falling under any of the above categories of BCs, they may, after
due diligence, approach the Regional Office of RBI at Guwahati for approval.
requirements.object: approach the Regional Office of RBI at Guwahati for approval

requirements.full_sentence:
The arrangements with the BCs shall specify:
(1) suitable limits on cash holding as also limits on individual customer payments
and receipts,
(2) the requirement that the transactions are accounted for and reflected in the
UCB's books by end of the day or next working day, and
(3) all agreements / contracts with the customer shall clearly specify that the UCB
is responsible to the customer for acts of omission and commission of the BF
/ BC.
requirements.object: on cash holding as also limits on individual customer payments and receipts, (2) the requirement that the transactions ar

requirements.full_sentence:
The distance between
the place of business of a BC and the base branch should not exceed 30 kms in
rural, semi-urban and urban areas and 5 kms in metropolitan centres.
requirements.object: rural, semi-urban and urban areas and 5 kms in metropolitan centres

requirements.full_sentence:
While
engaging BCs, the UCB shall ensure that the area covered by them is strictly
within their eligible area of operation.
requirements.object: covered by them is strictly within their eligible area of operation

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall conduct thorough due diligence of the entities proposed to be
appointed as BCs and also institute additional safeguards as may be considered
appropriate to minimise the agency risk.
requirements.object: of the entities proposed to be appointed as BCs and also institute additional safeguards as may be considered appropria

requirements.full_sentence:
The due diligence on entities to be
engaged as the BF / BC shall factor the key risks and other risks identified of
different capacities of entities.
requirements.object: and other risks identified of different capacities of entities

requirements.full_sentence:
Profit Orientation - Often MFIs / NGOs are social service
oriented, which may hamper undertaking a task like financial
intermediation.
requirements.object: like financial intermediation

requirements.full_sentence:
(vii) Secular and Social Orientation - The MFI / NGOs should preferably be
nondiscriminatory on caste, gender, political affiliation and religious lines.
requirements.object: caste, gender, political affiliation and religious lines

requirements.full_sentence:
it should
not have any negative discrimination.

(a) The federation should have a system of rating its constituent SHGs at
periodic intervals, preferably six monthly.
requirements.object: rating its constituent SHGs at periodic intervals, preferably six monthly

requirements.full_sentence:
At least 75 percent of the
SHGs should be rated on all the defined parameters.
requirements.object: the defined parameters

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The federation should be a registered body and have capacity to enter
into legal obligations.
requirements.object: and have capacity to enter into legal obligations

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) The federation should have a defined area of operation and have only
primary SHGs as members.
requirements.object: of operation and have only primary SHGs as members

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) The federation should have an elected board which should meet at
regular intervals.
requirements.object: which should meet at regular intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
(e) The accounts of the federation should have been audited and placed
before the General Body within six months of the closing of the
accounting period.
requirements.object: placed before the General Body within six months of the closing of the accounting period

requirements.full_sentence:
(f) The federation should have filed all the statutory returns before the
appropriate authority.
requirements.object: statutory returns before the appropriate authority

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) The federation should be in operating profit from the thrift and credit
operations.
requirements.object: from the thrift and credit operations

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The thrift collections at the SHG level should be at least 90 percent of
the determined amount.
requirements.object: percent of the determined amount

requirements.full_sentence: (c) The share capital mobilisation should not be in arrears.

(d) The federation should demonstrate a repayment performance of 90
percent or more on a continuous basis.
requirements.object: of 90 percent or more on a continuous basis

requirements.full_sentence: (e) At least 95 percent of the Loan Assets should be in performing category.
(f) The federation should have made adequate loan loss provisions.
requirements.object: loss provisions

requirements.full_sentence:
(g) The federation should have defined exposure norms for individuals and
groups.
requirements.object: for individuals and groups

conditions:
in case of other entities
In case of entities other than MFIs and NGOs
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Due Diligence in case of other entities
In case of entities other than MFIs and NGOs, a due diligence may have to be
more rigorous.
requirements.object: rigorous

requirements.full_sentence:
In engaging intermediaries as BCs, a UCB shall ensure that they are well
established, enjoying good reputation and having the confidence of the local
people.
requirements.object: well established, enjoying good reputation and having the confidence of the local people

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs may ensure that the individuals engaged as BCs are permanent
residents of the area in which they propose to operate as BCs and also include
additional safeguards as may be considered appropriate to minimise agency risk.
requirements.object: engaged as BCs are permanent residents of the area in which they propose to operate as BCs and also include additional saf

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB may give wide publicity in the locality about the intermediary engaged
by them as BC and take measures to avoid being misrepresented.
requirements.object: the locality about the intermediary engaged by them as BC and take measures to avoid being misrepresented

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, the duly appointed BCs of the UCB desire to appoint sub-agents at the
grass-root level to render the services of a BC, the UCB shall ensure that : (i) the
sub-agents of BCs fulfill all relevant criteria stipulated for BCs in terms of the
guidelines;
requirements.object: the sub-agents of BCs fulfill all relevant criteria stipulated for BCs in terms of the guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
and (iii) the distance criterion of 30 kms / 5 kms, as applicable, from the base
branch should invariably be fulfilled in the case of all sub-agents.
requirements.object: the case of all sub-agents

requirements.full_sentence:
Considering the profile of the clientele to whom banking
services are being delivered through the BC model, the UCB shall ensure that
the service charges / fees collected from the customer for delivery of banking
services through the BC model are not only fair and reasonable but also seen to
be so.
requirements.object: charges / fees collected from the customer for delivery of banking services through the BC model are not only fair and reasona

requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the Board-approved policy in this regard may be forwarded to
the concerned Regional Office of RBI.
requirements.object: concerned Regional Office of RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB shall ensure that there are no
complaints from the customers about the charges being non-transparent / not
reasonable.
requirements.object: no complaints from the customers about the charges being non-transparent / not reasonable

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB may pay reasonable commission / fee to the BFs / BCs, the rate and
quantum of which may be reviewed periodically.
requirements.object: fee to the BFs / BCs, the rate and quantum of which may be reviewed periodically

requirements.full_sentence:
The agreement with the BFs /
BCs shall specifically prohibit them from charging any fee to the customers
directly for services rendered by them on behalf of the UCB.
requirements.object: charging any fee to the customers directly for services rendered by them on behalf of the UCB

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery within the UCB for
redressing complaints about services rendered by BFs and BCs and give wide
publicity about it through electronic and print media.
requirements.object: within the UCB for redressing complaints about services rendered by BFs and BCs and give wide publicity about

requirements.full_sentence:
The name and contact
number of designated Grievance Redressal Officer of the UCB shall be widely
publicised and also placed in public domain.
requirements.object: also placed in public domain

requirements.full_sentence:
The details of the grievance
redressal officer shall be displayed at the premises of the BC and also at the base
branch.
requirements.object: premises of the BC and also at the base branch

requirements.full_sentence:
The designated officer shall ensure that grievances of customers are
redressed promptly.
requirements.object: customers are redressed promptly

requirements.full_sentence:
The grievance redressal procedure of a UCB and the time frame fixed for
responding to the complaints shall be placed on the UCB's website.
requirements.object: UCB's website

requirements.full_sentence:
Since the objective is to extend savings and loan facilities to the underprivileged
and unbanked population, the UCB may adopt a flexible approach within the
parameters of guidelines issued on KYC from time to time.
requirements.object: within the parameters of guidelines issued on KYC from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
As the engagement of intermediaries as BCs / BFs involves significant
reputational, legal and operational risks, due consideration shall be given by the
UCB to those risks.
requirements.object: UCB to those risks

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB should also endeavour to adopt technology-based
solutions for managing the risk, besides increasing the outreach in a cost-
effective manner.
requirements.object: technology-based solutions for managing the risk, besides increasing the outreach in a cost- effective manner

requirements.full_sentence:
The implementation of the BC / BF model shall be monitored closely by controlling
authorities of the UCB, who should specifically look into the functioning of BCs /
BFs during the course of their periodical visits to the branches.
requirements.object: controlling authorities of the UCB, who should specifically look into the functioning of BCs / BFs during the course of their

requirements.full_sentence:
In order to achieve greater penetration of banking service, the UCB may scale up
its efforts substantially towards educating its clientele through various means -
print, electronic etc., in their respective vernacular languages regarding the
benefits of banking habit, the role of the BCs and its obligation towards the
customers and give wide publicity about implementation of the BC model by
them.
requirements.object: substantially towards educating its clientele through various means - print, electronic etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Information regarding BCs engaged by the UCB shall be placed on the UCBs'
websites.
requirements.object: UCBs' websites

requirements.full_sentence:
The Annual Report of the UCB shall also include the progress in
respect of extending banking services through the BC model and the initiatives
taken by the UCB in this regard.
requirements.object: in respect of extending banking services through the BC model and the initiatives taken by the UCB in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
For streamlining cash management, the UCB may consider adopting 'Cash
Routes' (linking various BCs, which are in close proximity to each other to a base
branch) wherever warranted with suitable cash transit insurance.
requirements.object: (linking various BCs, which are in close proximity to each other to a base branch) wherever warranted with suitable ca

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB shall ensure the preservation and protection of the security and
confidentiality of the customer information in the custody or possession of the
BCs.
requirements.object: protection of the security and confidentiality of the customer information in the custody or possession of the BCs

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB may consider bearing the initial set up cost and other costs of the BCs
and extend a handholding support to the BCs, at least during the initial stages.
requirements.object: set up cost and other costs of the BCs and extend a handholding support to the BCs, at least during the initial stages

requirements.full_sentence: The UCB may consider providing reasonable temporary overdrafts to the BCs.
requirements.object: overdrafts to the BCs

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB may develop suitable training modules in the local language/s, in order
to provide proper attitudinal orientation and skills to the BCs.
requirements.object: in the local language/s, in order to provide proper attitudinal orientation and skills to the BCs

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB shall adhere to the extant RBI guidelines on adoption of appropriate
technology as contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks
- Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025, while implementing the BC
model.
requirements.object: RBI guidelines on adoption of appropriate technology as contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Managi

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide Doorstep Banking Services to its customers on a voluntary
basis, without prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to its customers on a voluntary basis, without prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may formulate a scheme for providing Doorstep Banking Services to
its customers, with the approval of their Boards, in accordance with the guidelines
provided in Annex V.
requirements.object: providing Doorstep Banking Services to its customers, with the approval of their Boards, in accordance with the guidelines prov

requirements.timeline: within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme
requirements.full_sentence:
The details of the scheme may be informed to the Reserve
Bank within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 3 and 4 UCBs may offer doorstep banking services to customers either
directly through its own employees or through agents.
requirements.object: to customers either directly through its own employees or through agents

requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 1 and 2 UCBs shall
offer doorstep banking services only through their permanent employees.
requirements.object: only through their permanent employees

requirements.full_sentence:
The
bank shall take into account the various risks that may arise on account of offering
Doorstep Banking Services, such as outsourcing risk, operational risk,
reputational risk, etc., to customers either directly through its own employees or
through agents and take all necessary steps to manage the same.
requirements.object: various risks that may arise on account of offering Doorstep Banking Services, such as outsourcing risk, operational risk, reput

requirements.full_sentence:
The operation of the scheme may be reviewed by the Board of the bank on an
annual basis.
requirements.object: Board of the bank on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Under this system, banks are required to submit their information in a single
Proforma (Annex I of CISBI) online on the CISBI portal.
requirements.object: in a single Proforma (Annex I of CISBI) online on the CISBI portal

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should submit information on
the CISBI portal as per guidelines given in Annex VI, and thereafter, bank branch
/ office / NAIO / CSP codes would be allotted by CISBI after due validations.
requirements.object: CISBI portal as per guidelines given in Annex VI, and thereafter, bank branch / office / NAIO / CSP codes would be allotted

requirements.full_sentence:
All banks should
submit immediately and, in any case, not later than seven calendar days, the
information relating to the opening, closure, merger, shifting and conversion of
bank branches / offices / NAIOs / CSPs online through the CISBI portal.
requirements.object: any case, not later than seven calendar days, the information relating to the opening, closure, merger, shifting and conver

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in order to ensure the correctness of data on CISBI, in the last week of
every month, banks shall generate a 'NIL Report' in CISBI for the position as on
last day of the previous month, indicating the total number of functioning
branches, offices, NAIOs, CSPs;
requirements.object: in CISBI for the position as on last day of the previous month, indicating the total number of functioning branches, offices,

requirements.full_sentence:
After
gaining first-time access to the system, banks shall ensure to submit correct and
updated bank-level information in all the fields where submission / updation rights
are available with the bank.
requirements.object: and updated bank-level information in all the fields where submission / updation rights are available with the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
After the initial submission of information on the CISBI
portal, a one-time confirmation stating that 'correct and updated Bank level
information has been submitted on CISBI' shall be sent by banks to the Regional
Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Any subsequent changes in the bank-
level information shall be submitted for updation on the CISBI portal on an
immediate basis by the banks.
requirements.object: on the CISBI portal on an immediate basis by the banks

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks shall enter the complete and clear
(without abbreviations) particulars on the Banking Channel Name, Address,
geotagging, etc.
requirements.object: clear (without abbreviations) particulars on the Banking Channel Name, Address, geotagging, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Reporting Timeline: Reporting on the CISBI portal within the prescribed time of
seven calendar days, shall be ensured by the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance for which a prior
approval of the Reserve Bank is not required for any authorisation in terms of
these Directions, the bank shall report such event / changes to the Reserve Bank
within 15 calendar days (reporting format given in Annex III).
requirements.object: changes to the Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days (reporting format given in Annex III)

requirements.full_sentence:
Point of contact for application / submission of information: The point of
contact for all matters related to this Direction shall be as under:
Sr.

All other UCBs Regional Office of the Reserve Bank under
whose jurisdiction the Head Office of the bank is
located
Note: Banks shall submit their applications to the Reserve Bank using relevant
application forms in the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in).
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank using relevant application forms in the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah

requirements.full_sentence:
The directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the
issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

1 CRAR should be at least one percentage
point above the minimum CRAR applicable
2 Net NPAs of not more than 3%
3 Net Profit during the last two financial years
4 Number of professional Directors on the
Board
No default in the maintenance of CRR / SLR during the
preceding and current financial year till the time of Board
resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant / application
for authorization
6 CBS fully implemented in the bank
7 Whether the bank is under Directions / Supervisory Action
Framework / PCA of RBI
8 Details, if any, of prohibition for any authorization by RBI
Enclosures:
requirements.object: percentage point above the minimum CRAR applicable 2 Net NPAs of not more than 3% 3 Net Profit during the last two financial years 4 Number of professional Directors on the Board No default in the maintenance of CRR / SLR during the preceding and current financial year till the time of Board resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant / application for authorization 6 CBS fully implemented in the bank 7 Whether the bank is under Directions / Supervisory Action Framework / PCA of RBI 8 Details

requirements.full_sentence:
Medium term policy for branch expansion programme: Bank may furnish details of
the proposed medium-term policy for its branch expansion for a period of 3 years along
with expected level of business in the next 3 years in terms of deposits and advances.
requirements.object: proposed medium-term policy for its branch expansion for a period of 3 years along with expected level of business in the next

requirements.full_sentence:
Details of proposed new branches
Name of the centre
Name of the
Members at the proposed
Population
with address and Pin
district and
centre (only for Salary Earners'
of the centre
code
state
Banks)
Note: Reasons for the proposed branch including adequacy of banking facilities at the
centre, business prospects at the proposed place of business within 12 months (an
estimate of minimum business which the bank expects to attract) may be submitted.

If yes,
details thereof may be submitted (attach extra sheet if necessary).
requirements.object: sheet if necessary)

requirements.full_sentence: Note: Separate statements may be submitted for each action.
requirements.object: action

requirements.full_sentence:
attached separately to each of the institutions, the
name and the distance of their bankers should also be indicated separately.)
∗ Strike out whichever is not applicable.

The banks which offer services of pick-up of cash shall take suitable steps to educate
their employees and agents to enable them to detect forged and mutilated notes so as
to avoid frauds and disputes with customers.
requirements.object: educate their employees and agents to enable them to detect forged and mutilated notes so as to avoid frauds and disputes with

conditions: Where banks engage the services of Agents for delivery of services
requirements.full_sentence:
Where banks engage the services of Agents for delivery of services, it should be
ensured that the policy approved by the Board lays down the broad principles for
selection of agents and payment of fee / commission etc.
requirements.object: policy approved by the Board lays down the broad principles for selection of agents and payment of fee / commission etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks must refer to the
Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing)
Directions, 2025 and ensure that the principles enumerated therein are complied with
while offering Doorstep Banking Services.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and ensure that the principles enumerat
a receipt on behalf of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
b) Cash collected from the customer should be credited to the customer's account
on the same day or next working day, depending on the time of collection;
requirements.object: customer's account on the same day or next working day, depending on the time of collection

requirements.full_sentence:
c) At the time of collection of cash, the customer should be informed of the date of
credit by issuing a suitable advice;
requirements.object: date of credit by issuing a suitable advice
to the account on the basis of requisition in writing / cheque received and not against cash or instruments collected at the doorstep

requirements.full_sentence:
e) Acknowledgment should be provided for collection of KYC documents, Life
Certificate.
requirements.object: of KYC documents, Life Certificate

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be ensured that the agreement entered into with the customer does not entail
any legal or financial liability on the bank for failure to offer doorstep services under
circumstances beyond its control.
requirements.object: agreement entered into with the customer does not entail any legal or financial liability on the bank for failure to offer doorste

requirements.full_sentence:
The services should be seen as a mere extension of
banking services offered at the branch and the liability of the bank should be the same
as if the transactions were conducted at the branch.
requirements.object: mere extension of banking services offered at the branch and the liability of the bank should be the same as if the transactions were con

requirements.full_sentence:
The agreement should not provide
any right to the customer to claim the services at his doorstep.
requirements.object: the customer to claim the services at his doorstep

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should provide cash limits (for collection as well as delivery) for their
employees / agents and customers, for doorstep banking.
requirements.object: collection as well as delivery) for their employees / agents and customers, for doorstep banking

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should also take
all necessary steps to contain technology risk while providing these services.
requirements.object: steps to contain technology risk while providing these services

requirements.full_sentence:
Charges, if any, to be levied on the customer for doorstep services should be
incorporated in the policy approved by the Board and should form part of the
agreement entered into with the customer.
requirements.object: policy approved by the Board and should form part of the agreement entered into with the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
The charges should be prominently
indicated on the banks' website and brochures offering doorstep services.
requirements.object: the banks' website and brochures offering doorstep services

requirements.full_sentence:
Other conditions
a) Banks shall ensure compliance with the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-
operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025 with regard to
customer identification procedures while offering doorstep services to their
customers.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co- operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025 with regard to customer identificat

requirements.full_sentence:
b) The services should be offered at either the residence or office of the customer
as opted by the customer, the address of which should be clearly and explicitly
mentioned in the agreement.
requirements.object: the residence or office of the customer as opted by the customer, the address of which should be clearly and explicitly mentioned

requirements.full_sentence:
c) The agreement / contract with the customer shall clearly specify that the bank
will be responsible for the acts of omission and commission of its 'agent'.
requirements.object: bank will be responsible for the acts of omission and commission of its 'agent'

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Banks shall keep in view the restrictions imposed by Section 10 (1) (b) (ii) of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, while making payments for the services
outsourced.
requirements.object: restrictions imposed by Section 10 (1) (b) (ii) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, while making payments for the services outsource

requirements.full_sentence:
Redressal of Grievance
a) Banks should constitute an appropriate Grievance Redressal Machinery
internally for redressing complaints about services rendered by its employees /
agents.
requirements.object: Redressal Machinery internally for redressing complaints about services rendered by its employees / agents

requirements.full_sentence:
The name and telephone number of the designated Grievance
Redressal Officer of the bank should be made available to the customers
including on the bank's website.
requirements.object: the customers including on the bank's website

requirements.full_sentence:
The designated officer should ensure that
genuine grievances of customers are redressed promptly.
requirements.object: of customers are redressed promptly

requirements.full_sentence:
Under this system, information related to Bank, Branches, Office, NAIOs, other fixed
customer service points (CSPs) (e.g., ATMs, etc.) must be submitted in CISBI.

All the UCBs are required to submit the above information in CISBI which will be
validated and published by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: information in CISBI which will be validated and published by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
For getting "Bank Admin ID", a bank
should provide an authorised email ID on which the Reserve Bank can forward "Bank
Admin ID" and its password in two different emails.
requirements.object: ID on which the Reserve Bank can forward "Bank Admin ID" and its password in two different emails

requirements.full_sentence:
A new UCB seeking reporting
access to CISBI, should contact the Reserve Bank with a request letter providing
details of the bank's nodal person, an email ID for receiving the login credentials and
certain basic documents as under:
a) Certificate of incorporation from the Registrar of Co-operative Societies / Central
Registrar of Co-operative Societies.
requirements.object: with a request letter providing details of the bank's nodal person, an email ID for receiving the login credentials and certa

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank should login on the CISBI portal (https://cisbi.rbi.org.in) using its allotted "Bank
Admin ID" and change the allotted password on the first login.
requirements.object: portal (https://cisbi

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank should fill all other information pertaining to the bank and submit on the CISBI
portal.
requirements.object: pertaining to the bank and submit on the CISBI portal

requirements.full_sentence:
For reporting any change in the existing information, banks should edit the existing
information and indicate the effective date of change.
requirements.object: and indicate the effective date of change

requirements.full_sentence: Banks must reset the password every three months.
requirements.object: three months

conditions:
If a bank finds any difference in the "Nil
Report" generated by CISBI and the actual status
requirements.full_sentence:
If a bank finds any difference in the "Nil
Report" generated by CISBI and the actual status, it should first update the information
in CISBI, then generate "Nil Report" and submit it through CISBI.
requirements.object: in CISBI, then generate "Nil Report" and submit it through CISBI

requirements.full_sentence:
In the last week of every month, banks shall generate 'NIL Report' for position as
on last day of previous month, authenticate it and submit on CISBI.
requirements.object: position as on last day of previous month, authenticate it and submit on CISBI

requirements.full_sentence:
For example, 'NIL
Report' for the month of June 2025 shall be generated and submitted in the last week
of July 2025.
requirements.object: in the last week of July 2025
